identity.name: SDL_RWLock
identity.titleArabic: A mutex that allows read-only threads to run in parallel

summary.actualTranslationArabic: A mutex that allows read-only threads to run in parallel
summary.actualMeaningArabic: يمثل هذا النوع نوع SDL RW Lock، أي القيمة أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي تتعامل معه بقية الواجهة في هذا السياق.
summary.purposeArabic: يستخدم هذا النوع عندما تحتاج القيمة أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي يمثله داخل بقية الواجهة.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا أنه يمثل نوع القيمة التي تتداولها بقية الواجهة بين الحقول والدوال.
summary.carriedValueArabic: يحمل هذا النوع القيمة الفعلية أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي تمرره بقية الدوال والحقول في هذا المسار.
summary.sourceDescription: A mutex that allows read-only threads to run in parallel.

remarks:
A rwlock is roughly the same concept as SDL_Mutex , but allows threads that request
read-only access to all hold the lock at the same time. If a thread
requests write access, it will block until all read-only threads have
released the lock, and no one else can hold the thread (for reading or
writing) at the same time as the writing thread.

This can be more efficient in cases where several threads need to
access data frequently, but changes to that data are rare.

There are other rules that apply to rwlocks that don't apply to
mutexes, about how threads are scheduled and when they can be
recursively locked. These are documented in the other rwlock
functions.
